rationale: The page contains detailed technical information about next-generation battery technologies, specifically Sodium-Ion Batteries (SIB), Vanadium Redox Flow Batteries (VRFB), and Solid-State Batteries (SSB). It includes specific performance metrics and comparative advantages suitable for generating multiple-choice questions.
cleanedText:
Sodium-Ion Battery (SIB)
* Research team working under Department of Science and Technology has developed a super-fast charging sodium-ion battery (SIB) that can charge up to 80% in just six minutes and last over 3000 charge cycles.
* About Sodium-Ion Battery (SIB)
* SIBs are a type of rechargeable battery similar to lithium batteries, but carry the charge using sodium ions (Na+) instead of lithium ions (Li+).
* Advantages of Sodium-Ion Batteries (SIBs) Compared to LIBs:
* Energy density: SIBs generally have lower energy density than Li-ion
* Safety: SIBs can be transported at zero voltage (fully discharged), reducing fire risks.
7.3. VANADIUM REDOX FLOW BATTERY
Why in the News?
India’s largest and first MWh-Scale Vanadium Redox Flow Battery (VRFB) inaugurated at NTPC NETRA.
More on the News
* Flow Batteries, along with solid state batteries, are one of the next generation battery technologies and are emerging as an alternative to lithium-ion for grid-scale applications.
About Next Generation Battery Technologies
* Flow Batteries: Flow batteries are powered by reduction-oxidation (redox) reactions and involve two different liquid electrolytes that pass ions or protons back and forth through a porous membrane.
* These batteries can store larger amounts of energy—as much as the size of the electrolyte cells can contain—and don’t use flammable or polluting materials.
* Types of Flow Batteries: Vanadium Redox, Zinc–Bromine, Iron–Salt/Organic systems.
* Solid-State Batteries: These use solid electrolyte solutions, which don’t need a different separator.
* SSBs have several advantages over conventional batteries:
* Improved safety where solid electrolytes are less prone to leakage or combustion compared to liquid electrolytes.
* Potential to store more energy in the same volume or weight.
* Capable of recharging up to 4–6 times faster than liquid electrolyte batteries, etc.
